title: SystemRelation
description: A typed edge between two type-level system entries. New in v0.2 — formalizes the freeform `links.yaml` files. Each entry's relations live at `registry/systems/<id>/relations.yaml` as a top-level YAML list of objects matching this schema. The edge is directed (source -> target); declare both directions explicitly when the relationship is mutual but asymmetric (e.g. competition strengths differ).

source_system.description: The relation source. Must match `id` of a `registry/systems/<id>/system.yaml`.

target_system.description: The relation target. Must match `id` of a `registry/systems/<id>/system.yaml`.

relation_type.description: Vocabulary from docs/framework/02 §28 and docs/framework/03 §21. Compositional: contains/part_of, depends_on. Functional: feeds, regulates, extracts_from. Competitive/cooperative: competes_with, cooperates_with, stabilizes, destabilizes. Modeling: models, simulates, copies. Historical: descends_from, replaces. Similarity: is_analogous_to (same function, different origin), is_homologous_to (shared deep structure or origin).

strength.description: Optional 0..1 strength score. Use sparingly — most type-level relations are categorical, not graded. Useful for `competes_with`/`cooperates_with` where intensity varies.

symmetry.description: Whether the relation is naturally bidirectional (e.g. `is_analogous_to` is symmetric — declare once with this flag) or directional (e.g. `part_of` — must be declared with explicit source/target).

dependency_type.description: Free-text qualifier on `depends_on` relations: e.g. `component`, `infrastructure`, `regulatory`, `informational`, `energetic`. Optional.

temporal_window.description: When the relation holds. Most type-level relations are timeless; use this to scope (e.g. `software-ecosystem depends_on internet-infrastructure since 1990s`).

confidence.description: Curator confidence in the relation. Default 1.0 for textbook relations (cell part_of organism); lower for analogies.

notes.description: Optional prose justifying the relation, especially for analogies and homologies where the comparison isn't obvious.
